text.title: embedding-models Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for embedding-models as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: OpenAI batch API returns embeddings in a list that may not be in input order; agents that skip the sort step get misaligned embeddings for shuffled batches.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The API does not guarantee order preservation. Always sort by response.data[i].index before pairing with input texts.

failure-mode.description: BGE-M3 lexical_weights output is a sparse dict per document, not a dense array — agents expecting list[float] will crash; handle both dense and sparse paths.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: BGE-M3 supports hybrid search with sparse weights. Agents must check the output type and handle sparse dict format differently from dense arrays.

failure-mode.description: SentenceTransformer model download at first use can take 30s-5min; agents in time-bounded tasks must pre-download models or use a local model cache.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: First call to model.encode() triggers a multi-gigabyte download from HuggingFace. Agents should pre-download during setup, not during the inference loop.

failure-mode.description: When using Matryoshka dimension reduction (dimensions param), the truncated vector is NOT simply the first N values of the full vector — it is separately trained; do not slice full-dim embeddings manually.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Matryoshka is a training technique that produces independent models at each dimension. Slicing full-dim embeddings produces garbage.

failure-mode.description: Voyage AI and Cohere have per-request token limits (varies by plan); agents must chunk texts before calling embed for texts >model context limit.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: API documentation specifies limits but does not enforce them with clear errors. Oversized requests silently truncate or fail with cryptic errors.

failure-mode.description: Sentence-transformers GPU inference requires matching CUDA/torch versions; a CUDA version mismatch silently falls back to CPU without an error.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: CUDA mismatch is detected at runtime but silently downgraded to CPU. Agent will see 10x slower inference and assume the model is just slow.
